message:
fix(grpc/codec): use saturating_add for header+length overflow safety

When a malformed gRPC frame carries a length field near usize::MAX, the
previous src.len() < MESSAGE_HEADER_SIZE + length expression could wrap
around, incorrectly concluding the buffer was large enough and leading
to an out-of-bounds slice. saturating_add clamps to usize::MAX, which
is always larger than any real buffer, so the incomplete-frame branch
fires correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
